kept.text:
⚡⚡⚡最新消息：據報導 SpaceX 擬在路易斯安那買 13.6 萬英畝建超級園區

👉 據當地房仲 Keaty Real Estate 的 Jim Keaty 報導，SpaceX 可能正計畫在路易斯安那州 Vermilion Parish 的 Pecan Island 與 Freshwater City 一帶，購置高達 13.6 萬英畝（約 212 平方英里）的超大土地，規模遠大於現有的德州 Starbase。

Keaty 列出三項可疑線索：當地狩獵租約異常變更、ExxonMobil 突然撤出該區開發案，以及地主收到遠高於市價的收購提案。

早前傳聞為 3 萬英畝，現規模翻了 4 倍以上，SpaceX 官方目前尚未證實。

✍️ 雖然消息尚未官方確認，但背景脈絡相當吻合：路州議會 2026 年確實已通過 HB 1088（航太銷售稅退稅）與 HB 1179（工業稅豁免延伸至航太製造），明確鎖定「投資 10 億美元以上」的航太公司。

這個地點具備三大優勢：偏遠、可駁船運輸火箭，並鄰近火箭燃料原料來源。加上紐奧良已有 NASA Michoud 製造基地（製造 Saturn V 與太空梭外掛油箱長達 60 年），路州的航太基礎完整。

不過這消息：「州長 Landry 復活節視察」目前僅匿名消息來源，獨立媒體尚未確認；SpaceX-xAI 合併後 IPO 計畫雖確有報導，但與路州土地無直接官方連結。整體仍屬「高度可信但未官方確認」。

去年１２月から注目しているエコスターはスペースXのIPO参加チケットになる

考えてみよう、IPO予定１．７兆USDの２％をエコスターが持っている

株価で換算すると約３４０億USDの価値がある、でエコスターの時価総額は？約３５０億USDほどだ。エコスターをただ同然で手に入れることができる

もちろんGoogleも６％ほど保有しているが、時価総額から見た時、大した金額ではない

エコスターの懸念されていた悪いファンダも下記の通り解消されているため、評価が変わって３月にSP500入りしたのも周知の事実

1. 借金（財務状況）の現状：劇的な「お色直し」
かつて200億ドル以上の純負債を抱えていた $SATS ですが、現在は以下のような「身軽な体質」に

２．債務再編の成功 (2026年3月): 債権者の82%以上の支持を得て、直近の支払い期限（満期）を2030年まで延ばすことに成功。これにより、「今すぐ現金が足りなくて倒産する」というリスクはほぼ消滅

３．借金の「肩代わり」: SpaceXとの提携により、SpaceXがエコスターの利払い（約20億ドル分）を2027年後半まで負担することに。これにより、エコスターは自前の現金を温存できるように。

４．ネットキャッシュの改善: 電波の売却（AT&TやSpaceXへ）により、将来的には約100億ドルのネットキャッシュ（正味の現金）を持つ計算になります。財務格付けも「投資適格（Junk脱却）」に向けて改善サイクルに入った。

今後の事業見通し：3つの柱
今後のエコスターは、「放送屋」から「宇宙インフラの大家さん」へ。

① Boost Mobile の「宇宙スマホ」化
SpaceXとの共同展開: 2026年2月から開始した、全米のBoost Mobile店舗（現在400店舗以上に拡大）でのStarlink連携サービスが好調。

差別化: 「空が見える場所ならどこでも繋がる」という、他社（VerizonやT-Mobile）が簡単には真似できない独自の強みを持つモバイルキャリアとして顧客を囲い込む。

② 企業向けサービス（HughesNet）の転換
法人・航空機特化: 苦戦していた個人向け衛星ネットから、デルタ航空などの航空機内Wi-Fiや政府・軍事向けのセキュア通信へと軸足を移しています。利益率の高い法人契約に絞ることで、売上の「質」を高める。

③ EchoStar Capital（投資部門）の始動
第2のSpaceX発掘: 前CEOのアカバン氏が率いる新部門で、潤沢になった資金とSpaceX株の含み益を背景に、次世代の宇宙技術企業への投資や買収を狙う。単なる事業会社から、宇宙産業の「投資会社」としての側面へ。

ネガティブな面: 事業の「売上高」自体は、放送事業の縮小により前年比で微減（-2.2%程度）する予測。しかし、「利益（EPS）」は87%以上の成長が見込まれており、「売れなくても儲かる構造」への転換が評価の鍵となる。

いづれにしてもSP５００に入ったということは、ステージが変わったとみてよい。

テクニカル的に見ても、底堅く戦争相場の時も横ばいで推移してきた。

また年足を見ても、他の宇宙と比べて、大きくあげてないところが、伸びしろを感じる部分だ。

いづれにせよ２０２６年末の当期純利益も黒字化予定。赤字から黒字化の瞬間が一番おもしろい。

最強の投機案件となることを願う。

年末予想株価は強気の２８０USDとしよう！！

※ほかにもエコスター以上に優良な宇宙銘柄２－３個ありますが、あくまでも、安いという観点で考察を書きました。
エコスターが上がる保証もなければ、これだけが絶対に良い銘柄というわけでもございません。

最終的に買うかどうかはご自身で決めなはれ。脳死ろんがー他責志向マンになる前に。

quoted_tweet.text:
衛星通信ネットワークの$エコスター A (https://t.co/HQDqjc5U28)$が上昇。アナリストが投資判断を「買い」に引き上げ、目標株価を１１８ドルに設定した。前日はマスク氏率いるスペースＸのＩＰＯ計画報道が株価を押し上げていた。
アナリストは、「無線通信キャリア間の競争激化により、同社が保有するスペクトラムの価値が一段と高まる」と指摘。特に、ベライゾンとＴモバイルＵＳが同社の残存するＡＷＳ－３帯域を積極的に取得しにくる可能性が高いと見ている。
年初来の株価上昇にもかかわらず、現在の時価総額は２０１４年の過去最高水準をなお下回っているとし、同社株には上値余地があると分析している。また、目標株価および強気シナリオでは、同社が保有するスペースＸ株を簿価（１株約４０ドル換算）で評価しているという。
なお、同社は先月に２億６０００万ドル相当のスペクトラムライセンスをスペースＸに売却することで合意。９月にも、総額約１７０億ドル規模の無線周波数帯をスペースＸへ現金および株式で売却する契約を締結している。

quoted_tweet.text:
Let me explain what just happened today because it deserves so much recognition.

GalaxEye is a Bengaluru startup founded in 2021 by IIT Madras engineers. Today they launched Mission Drishti on a SpaceX Falcon 9. It is India's largest privately built satellite at 190 kg. And it carries a technology that no commercial satellite has ever carried before.

Normal satellites take photos of the Earth using optical cameras. Like your phone camera, but from 500 km up. The problem is obvious. Clouds. Night. Fog. Smoke. If any of these are in the way, the photo is useless. India has monsoon cover for 4 months a year. That is 4 months where optical satellites are partially or fully blind over large parts of the country.

The alternative is SAR. Synthetic Aperture Radar. Instead of taking photos with light, it sends radar waves down and reads what bounces back. Radar goes through clouds, through darkness, through smoke. A SAR satellite can image a flooded village at 2 AM during a cyclone when no optical satellite can see anything.

The problem with SAR is that the images look nothing like photos. They look like grainy black-and-white radar maps. A military analyst or a trained geospatial engineer can read them. A farmer, a disaster response team, or a city planner cannot.

Until today, if you wanted both optical and SAR data for the same location, you needed two different satellites, passing over at different times, at different angles. Then someone had to manually align and fuse the two datasets. Expensive, slow, and the data never perfectly matched because the satellites saw the same spot minutes or hours apart.

GalaxEye put both sensors on one satellite. Optical and SAR, fused into what they call OptoSAR. Three times more information than a single sensor. Processed onboard by an NVIDIA AI chip at 1.8 metre resolution.

Now in practice, during the next cyclone hitting Odisha, one satellite pass gives you a clear image of which villages are flooded, which roads are cut, and which buildings are standing. Day or night. Cloud or clear. In near real-time.

For defence, it means you can monitor a border area 24/7 regardless of weather. For agriculture, it means tracking crop health across an entire monsoon season without a single cloud gap. For infrastructure, it means monitoring construction progress on highways and bridges without waiting for a clear day.

GalaxEye tested their SAR tech on ISRO's POEM orbital platform. The satellite was tested at ISRO facilities. IN-SPACe provided regulatory clearance. NSIL, ISRO's commercial arm, will distribute the imagery globally. And it launched on SpaceX because ISRO's PSLV doesn't have the right orbit slot for this mission.

Yes, four IIT Madras graduates built a world-first satellite in 4 years in Bengaluru. 

Take a bow!

kept.text:
Elon Musk:

“Building a new world on Mars is now possible.” https://t.co/fXE6jPeUQi

The math on this should mass-humble every aerospace program in history.

That stack is the most powerful machine humans have ever built. 33 Raptor 3 engines at the base, each producing 280 tons-force of thrust. Total liftoff thrust: 9,200 tons-force. The Saturn V that took us to the moon produced 3,580. Starship is 2.6x more powerful, 14 meters taller, and built to fly again.

Now look at the wheels.

Those are SPMTs, self-propelled modular transporters. The same machines used to salvage the 17,000-ton MV Sewol ferry from the East China Sea and decommission Shell's 20,300-ton Curlew oil platform. Each axle line holds up to 44 tons. SpaceX rolls them under Starship like furniture dollies.

Saturn V required a 526-foot Vehicle Assembly Building and a 6-million-pound crawler-transporter NASA built specifically for the Apollo program. Starship rolls out the side door of a building in Boca Chica on rented Mammoet equipment.

At the peak of Apollo, NASA built one Saturn V per year with 4.4% of the federal budget behind it. SpaceX has Ship 39 and Booster 19 ready for Flight 12, Ship 40 and Booster 20 under construction, and a Cape Canaveral facility built to produce enough heat shield tiles for 10 Starships per month.

5,300 tons fueled. 124 meters tall. Catches itself out of the sky with chopsticks.

The 1960s built one of these per year and the country watched it on TV. Boca Chica rolls them out at night.

🚀🇺🇸 SpaceX en Louisiane : un projet industriel hors norme se dessine dans les marais d’Acadiana.

Depuis plusieurs semaines, une rumeur persistante agite le sud de la Louisiane, entre discussions confidentielles et signaux sur le terrain. Même si, à ce jour, aucune confirmation officielle n’a été apportée, le croisement de sources locales et de données publiques suggère l’émergence d’un projet industriel d’envergure porté par SpaceX.

🔹 Dans la région d’Abbeville, jusqu’aux zones côtières de Pecan Island et Freshwater City, la rumeur évoque l’acquisition d’environ 55 000 hectares de marais. Cette information provient de témoignages locaux recueillis par l’agent immobilier Jim Keaty, qui indique avoir consulté élus, acteurs du secteur énergétique et résidents, sans démenti formel.

🔹 À titre de comparaison, cette surface d’environ 550 km² est comparable à celle de la Métropole de Lyon (environ 534 km²) et proche d’un petit département français comme le Territoire de Belfort (609 km²). Ce qui donne une idée concrète de l’échelle du projet…

🔹 L’un des fondements les plus solides repose sur un élément vérifiable : le projet de captage et stockage de carbone annoncé en 2022 par ExxonMobil sur environ 50 600 hectares dans le Vermilion Parish. Selon les registres publics, les demandes de permis ont été retirées en 2025, laissant un ensemble foncier structuré et potentiellement cessible.

🔹 À cela s’ajoutent des signaux de terrain, notamment sur les baux de chasse au sud de Pecan Island. Plusieurs sources locales indiquent une évolution des conditions d’accès dès la saison 2026, cohérente avec une reprise en main foncière préalable à une transaction industrielle.

🔹 Parallèlement, des propriétaires autour de Freshwater City rapportent des offres d’achat non sollicitées atteignant jusqu’à 10 fois la valeur estimée. Ces témoignages rappellent les méthodes observées lors du développement de Starbase au Texas, où SpaceX a sécurisé le foncier avant toute annonce.

🔹 La cohérence du projet repose aussi sur la géographie et les infrastructures, notamment la Gulf Intracoastal Waterway. Cette voie maritime permet le transport de structures industrielles de grande taille, et Freshwater City se situe à un point stratégique entre Boca Chica et Cape Canaveral.

🔹 L’environnement industriel régional renforce cette possibilité, avec la proximité du Cameron Parish, important hub américain pour le gaz naturel liquéfié. Les moteurs Raptor fonctionnant au méthane, cette ressource locale constitue un avantage logistique, complété par le renforcement du réseau électrique par Entergy.

🔹 Malgré ces éléments, l’analyse réglementaire impose une limite majeure. Selon les contraintes de la FAA, des lancements orbitaux vers l’est survoleraient des zones densément peuplées comme New Orleans, rendant improbable un pas de tir. L’hypothèse la plus crédible reste un complexe industriel dédié à la fabrication, aux essais moteurs et à la logistique.

🔹 Les implications économiques s’appuient sur le précédent de Starbase, où l’implantation de SpaceX a généré plusieurs milliards de dollars d’activité et une forte pression immobilière. Par extrapolation, Lafayette, Abbeville et Kaplan pourraient devenir des pôles résidentiels majeurs, avec une revalorisation rapide du foncier.

🔹 Enfin, l’analyse immobilière souligne que le corridor de la Highway 82 pourrait devenir un axe structurant, entre zones résidentielles, industrielles et logistiques. Ces conclusions proviennent des observations compilées par Jim Keaty, croisant données locales et dynamiques économiques.

👉 En conclusion, malgré l’absence de confirmation officielle, la convergence de témoignages, de données publiques et de logiques industrielles dessine un scénario crédible : celui de l’émergence d’un pôle aérospatial d’ampleur dans les marais d’Acadiana.

kept.text:
Let me talk about instant gratification and why it's destroying retail investors in $ASTS and honestly in life in general.

We live in a world where people can't wait for anything. Amazon same day delivery. DoorDash in 30 minutes. TikTok in 15 second clips. Swipe left, swipe right. Next. Next. Next. And people have taken that same energy into investing and it is genuinely one of the most destructive things I've ever watched happen in real time.

Here's what's wild to me. AST SpaceMobile went from $2 to $130. Let that sink in. $2 to $130. And now it's sitting at $74 and people are acting like the company is failing. You bought at $2 and you're complaining at $74? That's a 37x return and you're on X posting about how management lies and misses timelines? Be serious.

But let's talk about those timelines because this is where people really expose how little they understand about engineering, manufacturing, and what it actually means to build something that has never existed before.

AST SpaceMobile is not building an app. They're not dropping a software update. They are hand-building the most complex commercial satellites ever put into low earth orbit. Satellites so large, so technically advanced, that when you look at the BlueBird specs compared to anything else up there, it genuinely looks like science fiction. These things are pushing the absolute boundaries of what human engineering can do right now. Abel Avellan has been sleeping on the production floor. Let that image sit with you for a second. The CEO. Sleeping on the floor of the factory. Because that's the level of commitment happening behind the scenes while retail investors are posting bear takes from their couch.

And yes. They've missed timelines. You know who else missed timelines? Every single space company in the history of space. Every single one.

Elon Musk said people would be on Mars by 2025. It's 2026. Not even close. He's now saying 2030, which he'll probably miss too. SpaceX has missed more deadlines than I can count. Starlink missed timelines. Falcon Heavy missed timelines. Starship has been "almost ready" for years. And what's SpaceX worth right now? They're targeting a $2 TRILLION IPO valuation. TWO TRILLION DOLLARS. So the next time someone tells you ASTS missed a launch window so it's over, remind them that the greatest space company ever built missed virtually every major deadline they ever set and they're worth $2 trillion.

Amazon's Kuiper? Late. Blue Origin? Chronically late. New Glenn just put a BlueBird satellite in the wrong orbit. These are the best funded aerospace organizations on planet Earth and they can't hit a deadline. That's not failure. That's the nature of operating at the edge of what's physically possible.

What AST has accomplished is actually extraordinary when you look at it objectively. They went from a startup nobody believed in to having AT&T, Verizon, Rakuten, Orange, Telefonica, TELUS and 50+ of the world's largest mobile operators signed as partners serving nearly 6 billion subscribers. They got FCC approval for a 248 satellite constellation. They secured prime contractor status on a $151 billion Golden Dome defense program. They have $2B+ in cash, $1.2B in contracted commitments, and just filed a patent that could multiply their spectrum capacity by 3x to 10x making their already extraordinary spectrum position worth potentially $6.8B to $17B in added value in the US alone. And they did all of this while building something that has literally never been built before.

But the bears and the impatient want results now. Today. This quarter. And if they don't get it this quarter they're done. Moving on. Next.

That's not investing. That's gambling with an impatience problem layered on top.

Real investing requires understanding what you own, having conviction in the vision, and having the patience to let world-changing companies actually change the world. It took Amazon 10 years to be taken seriously. Netflix was a joke for years. Tesla was the most shorted stock on earth before it went up 10,000%. The pattern is always the same. Visionary company. Massive execution challenges in uncharted territory. Impatient retail investors exit. Institutions accumulate. Company delivers. Stock re-rates violently upward. Retail watches from the sideline.

Don't be that retail investor.

If you don't understand engineering timelines, if you've never built anything complex in your life, if you've never managed a manufacturing process for something that's never been made before, maybe pump the brakes before you post your bear take about a company whose CEO is sleeping on the factory floor to make sure a $30 million satellite is perfect before it goes to space.

Because once it goes up, it's up. Forever. You don't get to patch it. You don't get to recall it. You have one shot. And that's exactly why they take the time they take. And that's exactly why the bears will be wrong.

Patience isn't weakness. In investing, patience is the entire game.

$ASTS 🛰️

NFA

🚨 Tesla “Terafab”: a megascale semiconductor project set to reshape the AI era

1️⃣ Announced on March 21, 2026 by Elon Musk, Terafab is not just a factory but a vertically integrated AI chip manufacturing platform jointly pursued by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI. Musk described it as “the most ambitious chip manufacturing effort in history.”

2️⃣ The core objective is straightforward: secure overwhelming in-house compute production capacity to meet exploding AI demand. From FSD and Optimus to robotaxis, Starlink, and large-scale AI model training, the plan is to solve demand that existing foundries cannot keep up with.

3️⃣ While current global advanced foundry capacity is around 20GW annually, Terafab targets an unprecedented 1TW. That is roughly 50× today’s global output, a scale large enough to fundamentally reshape the industry if achieved.

4️⃣ The key differentiator is full vertical integration. Instead of fragmented design–manufacturing–packaging pipelines, Terafab aims to handle logic, memory, and advanced packaging within a single facility, reducing delays, costs, and geopolitical risks.

5️⃣ The site is planned north of Gigafactory Texas in Austin, Texas, with a structure expected to surpass even Gigafactory Texas in scale. Initial investment is estimated at $20–25 billion.

6️⃣ Production targets include 1 million wafers per month, 100–200 billion chips per year, and 1TW of annual AI compute output—roughly 10× the scale of a typical TSMC gigafab.

7️⃣ Technically, the facility is designed to integrate design, lithography, fabrication, testing, and packaging in one location, targeting sub-2nm processes alongside collaboration with Intel on 14Å (1.4nm) technology.

8️⃣ The chip portfolio spans beyond automotive, including edge inference chips for FSD and Optimus as well as space-grade, high-reliability chips for orbital infrastructure—effectively covering both terrestrial and space-based compute needs.

9️⃣ Strategically, Terafab represents the final piece of Musk’s vertically integrated ecosystem: from batteries to vehicles, energy, AI, robotics, and now semiconductors—minimizing external dependencies while controlling the full value chain.

🔟 Ultimately, Terafab is driven by a simple premise: without chips, there is no future. As compute underpins AI, robotics, autonomy, and space infrastructure, Terafab is less a factory and more a foundational layer for future civilization.
